pages.title: One Tap Away (Riverboat Bad Boys Remix)
pages.tagline: Tap once. Feel the noir silence between you.

pages.body: This mid-tempo 95 BPM boom bap track fuses grimy East Coast drums with dusty upright bass and sinister piano to paint a black-and-white 1928 riverboat world. Raspy vocals deliver a gritty critique on digital isolation, where social stats replace genuine intimacy amidst cinematic dockside crime. The song journeys from nostalgic reflection in the intro through cold observations of fractured contact, building into anthemic choruses that question why we feel alone surrounded by names. As intensity drops to resigned acceptance and heartbreaking clarity, the track exposes the vulnerability behind superficial fame and lost connections.
pages.styleSentence: A gritty hardcore gangsta rap track running at a mid-tempo 95 BPM that fuses grimy 90s East Coast boom bap drums with dusty upright bass stabs and sinister piano loops to create a black-and-white 1928 riverboat noir atmosphere, featuring raspy aggressive male vocals over an instrumentation palette of muted trumpets, creepy xylophone hits, vinyl hiss, and foghorn-like low-end frequencies.

pages.text:
[Intro]
[strummed acoustic guitar, punchy kick drum]

[Verse 1]
[rhythmic male rap vocals]
Ads in the cracks, brands in the chat
Everybody got stats, everybody wants claps
Everybody got reach but don't reach back
How we got this close and lost contact?

[Pre-Chorus]
[bass enters, melodic delivery]
You can see me in a second
You can call me any day
You can love me in private
But scroll right past my name
Funny how we got connected
Then forgot how to stay
You're right there in my pocket
But you feel a world away

[Chorus]
[full drums, anthemic vocals]
One tap away (one tap away)
From a real conversation but we don't say a thing
We're one tap away
From "I see you keep going" from "I'm proud of your dream"
Save your likes for the strangers
Save your hearts for the stars
But the ones who really know you act like loving you is hard
One tap away
So why do I feel alone in a room full of names?

[Verse 2]
[drums continue, rap delivery]
Tap, tap, but you don't reach back
Heart, heart, but it don't feel like that
Close, close, close, but we don't connect
One tap away and we still forget

[Bridge]
[acoustic guitar becomes more prominent]
Click, click, click, everybody got a new rule
Don't hype friends that ain't cool, cool
Save that love for the big names
Blue checks, big chains, big views
That if a stranger blow up overnight
Everybody's where they saw the light
Same ones quiet when you need a find
Now they in the comments like "that's my guy"

[Outro]
[drums drop out, fading acoustic guitar]
One tap away
And still so far
Everybody online
Nobody where you are

pages.vocalStyle: raspy street delivery with call-and-response ad-libs

members.name: LEAD VOX
members.bio: Raspy voice that cuts through the static noise.

members.name: BACKING VOX
members.bio: Harmonies that weave secrets into every line sung.

members.name: DRUMS
members.bio: Kick drums keep the gritty street rhythm alive.

members.name: PERCUSSION
members.bio: Creepy xylophone hits add playful danger to beats.

members.name: BASS
members.bio: Low-end frequencies rumble like a distant foghorn.

members.name: SYNTH
members.bio: Muted trumpets swell with cinematic melancholy.

members.name: TEXTURES
members.bio: Textures of vinyl hiss fill the digital void.
